title: Test Junk Scarecrow: 066-E02 SCHEDULE_RETURN_TO_HAND

plan.providerWarning: No WARD_LLM_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY found. The app will use local fallback plans until a server key is configured.

card.rawText: On hit, play this card from your hand. This card negates the attack and is sent to the cemetery instead. Return this card to your hand after 3 turn cycles.

plan.title: Test Junk Scarecrow: 066-E02 SCHEDULE_RETURN_TO_HAND
plan.summary: Create a deterministic dev scenario for Junk Scarecrow and verify that 066-E02 SCHEDULE_RETURN_TO_HAND resolves according to the parsed effect and WARD rules. Assertions were tightened from the printed card text for headless QA.

forcedRolls.label: Junk Scarecrow QA: stable attack damage

notes: Rules text: on hit, play Junk Scarecrow from hand to negate the attack and send this card to the cemetery instead; return it to hand after 3 turn cycles.
Run the battle-response branch first, then verify the delayed return branch.

steps: Player 1 attacks and reaches the on-hit response window.
Player 1 plays Junk Scarecrow from hand and sends it to the cemetery instead of taking attack damage.
Advance three turn cycles and verify Junk Scarecrow returns to hand.

expectedAssertions.label: Junk Scarecrow returned to hand after three turn cycles

manualVerification: Confirm the card is in cemetery during the delay and returns only after three full turn cycles.
riskNotes: Fallback plan cannot infer every WARD edge case.
The engine remains source of truth; the LLM/fallback only proposes a QA plan.

coverageSuggestion.notes: Run the generated scenario and update after reviewing result.

runnerNotes: Create a dev match from plan.setup, execute plan.steps, then verify plan.expectedAssertions.
The LLM only proposes the fixture. The deterministic engine and manual QA status remain source of truth.
